steps.title: Set the equations equal and simplify
steps.content:
From the second equation, $y = 5x - 8$. Substitute into $y = x^2 + 3x - 7$:
$$x^2 + 3x - 7 = 5x - 8$$
Collecting all terms on one side:
$$x^2 - 2x + 1 = 0$$

steps.title: Apply the discriminant test
steps.content: For $x^2 - 2x + 1 = 0$, we have $a = 1$, $b = -2$, $c = 1$. The discriminant is: $$\Delta = b^2 - 4ac = (-2)^2 - 4(1)(1) = 4 - 4 = 0$$ Since $\Delta = 0$, there is exactly one real solution. C is correct.
